content.introduction: US stock markets closed slightly lower amid renewed Iran tensions, with the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq showing mixed performance. Oil prices spiked, and markets assessed regional fallout.
body: # Stock Markets stumble from renewed Iran tensions – Dow Jones, Nasdaq and S&P 500 Intraday Outlook. Trading remains mixed and cautious as everyone waits for more news – Markets are, for now, beginning a move to the downside. With warning signs in the commodity market and most corporate earnings already reported, **traders are waiting for the next big economic event, so keeping track of the latest fundamental news will be particularly key for this week's trading.**. ***Let's dive into intraday charts and trading levels for the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Nasdaq Composite, and S&P 500.***. Current picture for the Stock Market (11:19) – Source: TradingView – May 4, 2026. ### Dow Jones 4H Chart and Trading Levels. Dow Jones (CFD) 4H Chart – May 4, 2026 – Source: TradingView. Nasdaq (CFD) 4H Chart – May 4, 2026 – Source: TradingView. ### S&P 500 4H Chart and Trading Levels. S&P 500 (CFD) 4H Chart – May 4, 2026 – Source: TradingView.

Business News›Markets›US Stocks›News›US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Highlights: US stocks end slightly lower amid renewed US-Iran tensions. # US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Highlights: US stocks end slightly lower amid renewed US-Iran tensions. Dow Jones Today | US Stock Market Highlights: U.S. stocks closed slightly lower on Monday, with each of the three major indexes coming off a third straight week of gains, as renewed U.S.-Iran tensions put the durability of a two-week ceasefire in doubt. Dow Jones Today | Nasdaq | S&P 500 | US Stock Market Live Highlights: The S&P 500 lost 15.84 points, or 0.22%, to end at 7,110.22 points, while the Nasdaq Composite lost 60.48 points, or 0.25%, to 24,408.00. ## US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Highlights: US stocks closes slightly down on renewed tensions between US, Iran. U.S. stocks closed slightly lower on Monday, with each of the three major indexes coming off a third straight week of gains, as renewed U.S.-Iran tensions put the durability of a two-week ceasefire in doubt.

# Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq avoid steep sell-off as oil spikes amid early Iran fallout. US stocks recovered from a substantial sell-off by the late morning on Monday and ended the day mostly higher, as Wall Street assessed the fallout from US and Israeli attacks on Iran as well as subsequent counterattacks. The S&P 500 (^GSPC) ended the day little changed, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC) gained 0.3% after both indexes rebounded from an early retreat. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (^DJI) fell 0.2% after recovering from more substantial losses. While Iran is the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries' (OPEC) fourth-largest producer, markets are also bracing for sustained disruption in the key Strait of Hormuz, where tanker traffic has come to a standstill. Stocks closed out mixed on Monday as oil surged and traders assessed the fallout from US and Israeli attacks on Iran and counterattacks in the region.
